title: Xtra Spicy Molten Argentinian Red Chilli Beef Empanadas
description: A flexitarian-friendly Argentinian dish featuring red chilli and beef in a molten empanada, served in a terracotta dish. This recipe combines umami flavors with a quick-fix approach.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Stir in the red chilli flakes, salt, black pepper, and paprika, and cook for 1 minute.
Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a spoonful of the beef mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Sprinkle shredded mozzarella cheese over the beef mixture.
Fold the dough in half over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with beaten eggs and place them in a terracotta dish.
Bake the empanadas in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F.
